data_description.connection_type: Local MQTT uses your existing MQTT broker for real-time local control. Cloud uses the Häfele cloud API.

data_description.use_ha_mqtt: Use the MQTT broker configured in Home Assistant. Disable to connect to a different broker.
data_description.topic_prefix: The MQTT topic prefix configured in your Häfele Gateway (default: hafele)
data_description.add_group_entities: Expose Häfele light groups as separate light entities in Home Assistant.

data_description.broker: Hostname or IP address of your MQTT broker
data_description.username: Leave empty if authentication is not required
data_description.password: Leave empty if authentication is not required

data_description.api_token: Generate an API token at https://cloud.connect-mesh.io/developer after creating a Connect Mesh Cloud account.

network.title: Select Network
network.description:
Found {device_count} devices across all networks.
Select a network to continue...

data_description.network_id: Select the Häfele Connect Mesh network whose devices you want to add to Home Assistant.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate Integration
reauth_confirm.description: The Häfele Connect Mesh integration needs to reauthenticate your account

data_description.api_token: Enter a valid API token to restore the connection. Generate one at https://cloud.connect-mesh.io/developer.

error.mqtt_not_configured: MQTT integration is not configured. Please add the MQTT integration first.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to poll device state from the cloud API (seconds). Default: 30 s.
data_description.device_details_update_interval: How often to check for device name changes (minutes). Default: 5 min.
data_description.new_devices_check_interval: How often to check for newly added devices (minutes). Default: 15 min.

data_description.add_group_entities: Expose Häfele light groups as separate light entities in Home Assistant.
